code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent, combined sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a formatting lambda (subject, object) -> phrase.
    # For predicates that naturally form a relative clause we omit the subject.
    predicate_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"formerly played for {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"is coached by {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"was the {o}th pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"debuted with {o}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"is led by {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"is based in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"owned by {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"chaired by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"managed by {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"plays at {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"plays at {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"has anthem {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"has language {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"has official language {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"has currency {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"has demonym {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"was founded on {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"covers an area of {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"is in the {o} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"season is {o}",
    }

    # Build phrases, grouping repeated subjects for certain predicates (e.g., club)
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        key = (t.subject, t.predicate)
        grouped.setdefault(key, []).append(t.object)

    sentences = []
    for (subj, pred), objects in grouped.items():
        if pred in {"club", "currentclub"} and len(objects) > 1:
            # List multiple clubs
            objs = ", ".join(objects[:-1]) + f", and {objects[-1]}" if len(objects) > 2 else " and ".join(objects)
            phrase = f"{subj} plays for {objs}"
        else:
            # Use the first object for simple predicates
            obj = objects[0]
            phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)
        sentences.append(phrase)

    # Combine sentences into a single fluent paragraph
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0] + "."
    *head, tail = sentences
    combined = ", ".join(head) + ", and " + tail
    return combined + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
